extraction.one_line_summary: COBRELLI Sonny won the uphill sprint finish after ARANBURU Alex (Astana) jumped too early on the left side, while MCNULTY Brandon closed the gap to preserve POSTELBERGER's yellow jersey.
tactical_patterns: Astana's ARANBURU jumped left-side early in uphill sprint, creating gap before fading—premature acceleration pattern on slight gradients.
UAE's MCNULTY closed Astana's sprint gap in final meters, preserving teammate POSTELBERGER's GC by denying COBRELLI time bonus.
Bahrain failed sprint leadouts on Stages 1-2 despite fastest sprinter; finally delivered Stage 3 after breakaway absorption.
STUYVEN working for POSTELBERGER's GC defense rather than PEDERSEN sprint, revealing Trek's Stage 3 priority hierarchy.

rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: positive: finished third in uphill sprint beating pure sprinters KRISTOFF/JACOBSEN—significant power signal before next-day TT
rider_signals.COBRELLI Sonny: positive: proved strongest sprinter across three stages but team execution failed twice before Stage 3 delivery
rider_signals.ARANBURU Alex: negative: mistimed early sprint acceleration on left side, opened gap but couldn't sustain to finish
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: described as 'pedaling squares' and disorganized in final sprint positioning
